title: cycas circinalis subsp. papuana
intro: Cycas circinalis subsp. papuana is a sub-species of the Cycas circinalis plant, commonly known as the sago palm or cycad, which is a type of gymnosperm that produces cones and has been in existence for millions of years. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and seeds of the plant are said to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The plant's leaves and seeds are often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to have a range of health benefits and are used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
